token.\npub fn apply_retained_amendments(\n    prompt: &str,\n    answer: &mut SymbolicAnswer,\n    events: &[MemoryEvent],\n) {\n    let additions = matching_amendment_lines(prompt, events);\n    if additions.is_empty() {\n        return;\n    }\n    answer.answer.push_str(\"\\n\\n\");\n    answer.answer.push_str(&additions.join(\"\\n\"));\n    for amendment in retained_amendments(events)\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter(|amendment| topic_matches(prompt, &amendment.topic))\n    {\n        answer\n            .evidence_links\n            .push(format!(\"meta_algorithm_amendment:{}\", amendment.id));\n    }\n}\n\n/// Apply retained requirements to a plain final answer, including agentic\n/// final responses that do not pass through [`SymbolicAnswer`].\n#[must_use]\npub fn amended_answer(prompt: &str, answer: &str, events: &[MemoryEvent]) -> String {\n    let additions = matching_amendment_lines(prompt, events);\n    if additions.is_empty() {\n        answer.to_owned()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{answer}\\n\\n{}\", additions.join(\"\\n\"))\n    }\n}\n\nfn matching_amendment_lines(prompt: &str, events: &[MemoryEvent]) -> Vec<String> {\n    retained_amendments(events)\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter(|amendment| topic_matches(prompt, &amendment.topic))\n        .map(|amendment| {\n            format!(\n                \"Learned standing requirement ({}): {}\",\n                amendment.topic, amendment.rule\n            )\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn structured_value<'a>(value: Option<&'a str>, key: &str) -> Option<&'a str> {\n    let value = value?;\n    value\n        .strip_prefix(key)\n        .and_then(|tail| tail.strip_prefix('='))\n        .or(Some(value))\n}\n\nfn topic_matches(prompt: &str, topic: &str) -> bool {\n    let prompt = prompt.to_lowercase();\n    let topic = topic.to_lowercase();\n    if topic.is_empty() {\n        return false;\n    }\n    prompt.match_indices(&topic).any(|(start, matched)| {\n        let end = start + matched.len();\n        let left_ok = start == 0\n            || prompt[..start]\n                .chars()\n                .next_back()\n                .is_none_or(|character| !character.is_alphanumeric());\n        let right_ok = end == prompt.len()\n            || prompt[end..]\n                .chars()\n                .next()\n                .is_none_or(|character| !character.is_alphanumeric());\n        left_ok && right_ok\n    })\n}\n//! Candidate-task replay and language-independent recurring-structure mining.\n\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};\nuse std::fmt::Write as _;\n\nuse super::{\n    DreamingCandidateTask, DreamingEventObservation, DreamingPattern, LearnedRequirement,\n    MetaAlgorithmAmendment, TopicFrequency,\n};\nuse crate::memory::MemoryEvent;\nuse crate::solver::UniversalSolver;\n\npub(super) struct LearningResult {\n    pub topics: Vec<TopicFrequency>,\n    pub requirements: Vec<LearnedRequirement>,\n    pub amendments: Vec<MetaAlgorithmAmendment>,\n    pub candidate_tasks: Vec<DreamingCandidateTask>,\n    pub patterns: Vec<DreamingPattern>,\n}\n\npub(super) fn learn_from_memory(\n    events: &[MemoryEvent],\n    observations: &[DreamingEventObservation],\n    deleted_conversations: &BTreeSet<String>,\n) -> LearningResult {\n    let mut interactions: BTreeMap<String, usize> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut task_counts: BTreeMap<String, usize> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut requirement_counts: BTreeMap<String, usize> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut requirements: BTreeMap<(String, String), LearnedRequirement> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut specific_indices: BTreeMap<String, Vec<usize>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for (index, event) in events.iter().enumerate() {\n        if event\n            .conversation_id\n            .as_deref()\n            .is_some_and(|id| deleted_conversations.contains(id))\n        {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let Some(topic) = observations[index].topic.clone() else {\n            continue;\n        };\n        *interactions.entry(topic.clone()).or_default() += 1;\n        if is_task_event(event) {\n            *task_counts.entry(topic.clone()).or_default() += 1;\n            if observations[index].durability.is_reclaimable() && !event.id.is_empty() {\n                specific_indices\n                    .entry(topic.clone())\n                    .or_default()\n                    .push(index);\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(statement) = requirement_statement(event) {\n            *requirement_counts.entry(topic.clone()).or_default() += 1;\n            let entry = requirements\n                .entry((topic.clone(), statement.to_lowercase()))\n                .or_insert_with(|| LearnedRequirement {\n                    topic: topic.clone(),\n                    statement: statement.clone(),\n                    source_event_ids: Vec::new(),\n                    occurrences: 0,\n                });\n            entry.occurrences += 1;\n            if !event.id.is_empty() {\n                entry.source_event_ids.push(event.id.clone());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    let mut topics = interactions\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(topic, interactions)| TopicFrequency {\n            topic: topic.clone(),\n            interactions: *interactions,\n            task_events: task_counts.get(topic).copied().unwrap_or(0),\n            requirement_events: requirement_counts.get(topic).copied().unwrap_or(0),\n        })\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n    topics.sort_by(|left, right| {\n        right\n            .interactions\n            .cmp(&left.interactions)\n            .then_with(|| left.topic.cmp(&right.topic))\n    });\n    let requirements = requirements.into_values().collect::<Vec<_>>();\n    let amendments = generalize_amendments(events, &requirements, &specific_indices);\n    let candidate_tasks = replay_candidate_tasks(events, observations, &amendments);\n    let patterns = mine_patterns(events, observations);\n    LearningResult {\n        topics,\n        requirements,\n        amendments,\n        candidate_tasks,\n        patterns,\n    }\n}\n\npub(super) fn event_topic(event: &MemoryEvent) -> Option<String> {\n    for raw in [\n        event.conversation_title.as_deref(),\n        event.demo_label.as_deref(),\n        event.intent.as_deref(),\n        event.tool.as_deref(),\n    ]\n    .into_iter()\n    .flatten()\n    {\n        let normalized = raw.trim().to_lowercase();\n        if !matches!(\n            normalized.as_str(),\n            \"\" | \"message\"\n                | \"ask\"\n                | \"reply\"\n                | \"chat\"\n                | \"note\"\n                | \"generalize\"\n                | \"system\"\n                | \"user\"\n                | \"assistant\"\n        ) {\n            return Some(normalized);\n        }\n    }\n    None\n}\n\nfn requirement_statement(event: &MemoryEvent) -> Option<String> {\n    if event.role.as_deref().is_some_and(|role| {\n        role.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"assistant\") || role.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"system\")\n    }) {\n        return None;\n    }\n    let content = event.content.as_deref()?.trim();\n    let lowered = content.to_lowercase();\n    requirement_cues()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|cue| lowered.contains(cue))\n        .then(|| content.to_owned())\n}\n\nfn requirement_cues() -> Vec<String> {\n    include_str!(\"../../data/meta/dreaming-cues.lino\")\n        .lines()\n        .filter_map(|line| line.trim().strip_prefix(\"cue \\\"\")?.strip_suffix('\"'))\n        .map(str::to_lowercase)\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn generalize_amendments(\n    events: &[MemoryEvent],\n    requirements: &[LearnedRequirement],\n    specific_indices: &BTreeMap<String, Vec<usize>>,\n) -> Vec<MetaAlgorithmAmendment> {\n    let mut by_topic: BTreeMap<String, Vec<&LearnedRequirement>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for requirement in requirements {\n        by_topic\n            .entry(requirement.topic.clone())\n            .or_default()\n            .push(requirement);\n    }\n    by_topic\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(|(topic, requirements)| {\n            let indices = specific_indices.get(&topic)?;\n            let rule = requirements\n                .iter()\n                .map(|requirement| requirement.statement.as_str())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"; \");\n            let mut amendment = MetaAlgorithmAmendment {\n                id: crate::engine::stable_id(\"amendment\", &topic),\n                topic,\n                rule,\n                source_requirement_ids: requirements\n                    .iter()\n                    .flat_map(|requirement| requirement.source_event_ids.iter().cloned())\n                    .collect(),\n                covered_event_ids: Vec::new(),\n            };\n            amendment.covered_event_ids = indices\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|index| amendment_reproduces_specific(&amendment, &events[**index]))\n                .map(|index| events[*index].id.clone())\n                .collect();\n            Some(amendment)\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn amendment_reproduces_specific(amendment: &MetaAlgorithmAmendment, event: &MemoryEvent) -> bool {\n    let (Some(input), Some(expected)) = (event.inputs.as_deref(), event.outputs.as_deref()) else {\n        return false;\n    };\n    let simulated = simulate_candidate_output(input, &amendment.topic, [amendment]);\n    normalize(&simulated) == normalize(expected)\n}\n\nfn replay_candidate_tasks(\n    events: &[MemoryEvent],\n    observations: &[DreamingEventObservation],\n    amendments: &[MetaAlgorithmAmendment],\n) -> Vec<DreamingCandidateTask> {\n    events\n        .iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .filter(|(_, event)| is_task_event(event))\n        .filter_map(|(index, event)| {\n            let topic = observations[index].topic.as_deref()?;\n            let input = event.inputs.as_deref()?;\n            let expected = event.outputs.as_deref()?;\n            let matching = amendments\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|amendment| amendment.topic == topic)\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n            let simulated = simulate_candidate_output(input, topic, matching);\n            Some(DreamingCandidateTask {\n                topic: topic.to_owned(),\n                source_event_id: event.id.clone(),\n                input: input.to_owned(),\n                expected_output: expected.to_owned(),\n                passed: normalize(&simulated) == normalize(expected),\n                simulated_output: simulated,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn simulate_candidate_output<'a>(\n    input: &str,\n    topic: &str,\n    amendments: impl IntoIterator<Item = &'a MetaAlgorithmAmendment>,\n) -> String {\n    let mut simulated = UniversalSolver::default().solve(input).answer;\n    for amendment in amendments {\n        let _ = write!(\n            simulated,\n            \"\\n\\nLearned standing requirement ({topic}): {}\",\n            amendment.rule\n        );\n    }\n    simulated\n}\n\nfn mine_patterns(\n    events: &[MemoryEvent],\n    observations: &[DreamingEventObservation],\n) -> Vec<DreamingPattern> {\n    let mut groups: BTreeMap<(String, String), Vec<String>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for (index, event) in events.iter().enumerate() {\n        if !is_task_event(event) {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let (Some(topic), Some(input)) = (\n            observations[index].topic.as_deref(),\n            event.inputs.as_deref(),\n        ) else {\n            continue;\n        };\n        let Some(head) = input.split_whitespace().next() else {\n            continue;\n        };\n        let structure = format!(\"{} *\", head.to_lowercase());\n        groups\n            .entry((topic.to_owned(), structure))\n            .or_default()\n            .push(event.id.clone());\n    }\n    groups\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter(|(_, ids)| ids.len() >= 2)\n        .map(|((topic, structure), source_event_ids)| DreamingPattern {\n            topic,\n            structure,\n            occurrences: source_event_ids.len(),\n            source_event_ids,\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\nfn is_task_event(event: &MemoryEvent) -> bool {\n    let kind = event.kind.as_deref().unwrap_or_default().to_lowercase();\n    let intent = event.intent.as_deref().unwrap_or_default().to_lowercase();\n    [\"task\", \"test_run\", \"test-run\", \"trial\", \"run_log\"]\n        .iter()\n        .any(|cue| kind.contains(cue))\n        || [\"task\", \"solve\", \"test\"]\n            .iter()\n            .any(|cue| intent.contains(cue))\n}\n\nfn normalize(value: &str) -> String {\n    value.split_whitespace().collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\" \")\n}\n                DreamingAction {\n                    kind,\n                    event_id: events[event_index].id.clone(),\n                    conversation_id: events[event_index].conversation_id.clone(),\n                    estimated_bytes: observations[event_index].estimated_bytes,\n                    usage_count: observations[event_index].usage_count,\n                    reason,\n                },\n            );\n            selected_reclaim_bytes = selected_bytes(&actions);\n        }\n    }\n\n    selected_reclaim_bytes = selected_bytes(&actions);\n    DreamingPlan {\n        daydreaming_enabled: true,\n        target_free_ratio_percent,\n        storage_capacity_bytes: config.storage_capacity_bytes,\n        free_bytes: config.free_bytes,\n        incoming_bytes: config.incoming_bytes,\n        target_free_bytes,\n        required_reclaim_bytes,\n        selected_reclaim_bytes,\n        total_reclaimable_bytes,\n        requires_bigger_storage: required_reclaim_bytes > selected_reclaim_bytes,\n        actions,\n        observations,\n        topics,\n        learned_requirements,\n        amendments,\n        candidate_tasks,\n        patterns,\n    }\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn apply_dreaming_plan(store: &mut MemoryStore, plan: &DreamingPlan) -> DreamingOutcome {\n    let selected_ids = plan\n        .actions\n        .iter()\n        .map(|action| action.event_id.as_str())\n        .collect::<BTreeSet<_>>();\n\n    // Bake each learned generalization into memory as a retained learning record\n    // *before* forgetting the specifics it covers. Applying an unchanged plan\n    // twice must not duplicate amendments, so we skip ids already present.\n    let existing_ids: BTreeSet<String> = store\n        .events()\n        .iter()\n        .map(|event| event.id.clone())\n        .collect();\n    let new_amendments: Vec<MemoryEvent> = plan\n        .amendments\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|amendment| !existing_ids.contains(&amendment.id))\n        .map(amendment_event)\n        .collect();\n    let learned_amendments = new_amendments.len();\n    let new_patterns = plan\n        .patterns\n        .iter()\n        .map(pattern_event)\n        .filter(|event| !existing_ids.contains(&event.id))\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n    let learned_patterns = new_patterns.len();\n\n    if selected_ids.is_empty() && new_amendments.is_empty() && new_patterns.is_empty() {\n        return DreamingOutcome {\n            removed_events: 0,\n            estimated_reclaimed_bytes: 0,\n            learned_amendments: 0,\n            learned_patterns: 0,\n        };\n    }\n\n    let initial_len = store.len();\n    let mut retained = store\n        .events()\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|event| !selected_ids.contains(event.id.as_str()))\n        .cloned()\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n    let removed_events = initial_len - retained.len();\n    retained.extend(new_amendments);\n    retained.extend(new_patterns);\n    *store = MemoryStore::from_events(retained);\n    DreamingOutcome {\n        removed_events,\n        estimated_reclaimed_bytes: selected_bytes(&plan.actions),\n        learned_amendments,\n        learned_patterns,\n    }\n}\n\nfn pattern_event(pattern: &DreamingPattern) -> MemoryEvent {\n    let identity = format!(\"{}\\0{}\", pattern.topic, pattern.structure);\n    MemoryEvent {\n        id: crate::engine::stable_id(\"dreaming_pattern\", &identity),\n        kind: Some(String::from(\"dreaming_pattern\")),\n        role: Some(String::from(\"system\")),\n        intent: Some(String::from(\"generalize\")),\n        inputs: Some(format!(\"topic={}\", pattern.topic)),\n        outputs: Some(format!(\"structure={}\", pattern.structure)),\n        content: Some(format!(\n            \"Recurring structure {} observed {} time(s)\",\n            pattern.structure, pattern.occurrences\n        )),\n        demo_label: Some(pattern.topic.clone()),\n        evidence: pattern.source_event_ids.clone(),\n        ..MemoryEvent::default()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Render a learned generalization as a durable, never-reclaimable memory event.\nfn amendment_event(amendment: &MetaAlgorithmAmendment) -> MemoryEvent {\n    MemoryEvent {\n        id: amendment.id.clone(),\n        kind: Some(String::from(\"meta_algorithm_amendment\")),\n        role: Some(String::from(\"system\")),\n        intent: Some(String::from(\"generalize\")),\n        content: Some(amendment.rule.clone()),\n        inputs: Some(format!(\"topic={}\", amendment.topic)),\n        outputs: Some(format!(\"rule={}\", amendment.rule)),\n        demo_label: Some(amendment.topic.clone()),\n        conversation_title: Some(amendment.topic.clone()),\n        evidence: amendment\n            .source_requirement_ids\n            .iter()\n            .cloned()\n            .chain([String::from(\"recipe:data/meta/dreaming-recipe.lino\")])\n            .collect(),\n        ..MemoryEvent::default()\n    }\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn render_dreaming_plan(plan: &DreamingPlan) -> String {\n    let mut lines = Vec::new();\n    lines.push(String::from(\"memory_dreaming_plan\"));\n    lines.push(format!(\"  enabled: {}\", plan.daydreaming_enabled));\n    lines.push(format!(\n//! Portable Links Notation memory log shared across every interface.\n//!\n//! The browser demo persists its conversation memory under `IndexedDB` as a\n//! `demo_memory` Links Notation document (see `src/web/memory.js`). The CLI\n//! and the HTTP server reuse the **exact same wire format** so a user can\n//! migrate their agent's memory between surfaces with a single `.lino`\n//! file:\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! demo_memory\n//!   event \"id1\"\n//!     role \"user\"\n//!     content \"Hi\"\n//!     sentAt \"2026-05-15T12:00:00.000Z\"\n//!   event \"id2\"\n//!     role \"assistant\"\n//!     intent \"greeting\"\n//!     content \"Hi, how may I help you?\"\n//!     sentAt \"2026-05-15T12:00:01.000Z\"\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The store is append-only for normal writes. Destructive paths are explicit\n//! user-initiated maintenance operations: purge already-deleted conversations\n//! or reset the dynamic event log after the caller has handled confirmation /\n//! backup. Older logs without the optional `kind`/`tool`/`inputs`/`outputs`\n//! fields still parse as plain user/assistant turns, so the format is\n//! forward-compatible.\n//!\n//! Full-memory bundles (`formal_ai_bundle`) — seed files + UI preferences +\n//! environment metadata + the entire event log in a single document — live in\n//! the [`bundle`] submodule. They are the default shape every \"export memory\"\n//! surface now writes (see issue #18 / R109).\n//!\n//! See [`super::seed`] for the static knowledge surface that pairs with this\n//! dynamic memory log, and `VISION.md` (Single-File Reproducibility) for the\n//! reasoning behind the unified format.\n\nuse std::collections::BTreeSet;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\npub mod bundle;\n\npub use bundle::{\n    export_bundle, export_full_memory, extract_memory_from_bundle, import_full_memory,\n    suggest_migrations, BundleInfo, ParsedBundle,\n};\n\npub(crate) const ROOT_HEADER: &str = \"demo_memory\";\npub(crate) const BUNDLE_HEADER: &str = \"formal_ai_bundle\";\n\n/// One recorded turn / step / tool invocation.\n///\n/// All fields are optional so the same record shape covers user/assistant\n/// messages, internal reasoning steps, and tool invocations without\n/// branching the schema.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct MemoryEvent {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub kind: Option<String>,\n    pub role: Option<String>,\n    pub intent: Option<String>,\n    pub tool: Option<String>,\n    pub inputs: Option<String>,\n    pub outputs: Option<String>,\n    pub content: Option<String>,\n    pub sent_at: Option<String>,\n    pub demo_label: Option<String>,\n    pub conversation_id: Option<String>,\n    pub conversation_title: Option<String>,\n    pub evidence: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nimpl MemoryEvent {\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn user(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            role: Some(String::from(\"user\")),\n            content: Some(content.into()),\n            ..Self::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn assistant(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            role: Some(String::from(\"assistant\")),\n            content: Some(content.into()),\n            ..Self::default()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Memory log for dynamic events. Normal writes append records; explicit purge\n/// and reset methods exist for irreversible user-requested cleanup.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct MemoryStore {\n    events: Vec<MemoryEvent>,\n}\n\nimpl MemoryStore {\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn new() -> Self {\n        Self { events: Vec::new() }\n    }\n\n    /// Build a store from an existing list. Useful for tests and for the\n    /// `from_links_notation` factory below.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn from_events(events: Vec<MemoryEvent>) -> Self {\n        Self { events }\n    }\n\n    pub fn append(&mut self, event: MemoryEvent) {\n        self.events.push(event);\n    }\n\n    /// Append every event from `other` to this store. Returns the number of\n    /// events appended.\n    pub fn import(&mut self, other: &[MemoryEvent]) -> usize {\n        let initial = self.events.len();\n        self.events.extend_from_slice(other);\n        self.events.len() - initial\n    }\n\n    /// Permanently remove all events that belong to conversations already\n    /// marked with a `conversation_deleted` event.\n    pub fn purge_deleted_conversations(&mut self) -> usize {\n        let deleted_ids: BTreeSet<String> = self\n            .events\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|event| event.kind.as_deref() == Some(\"conversation_deleted\"))\n            .filter_map(|event| event.conversation_id.as_deref())\n            .map(ToOwned::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        if deleted_ids.is_empty() {\n            return 0;\n        }\n        let initial = self.events.len();\n        self.events.retain(|event| {\n            event\n                .conversation_id\n                .as_deref()\n                .is_none_or(|id| !deleted_ids.contains(id))\n        });\n        initial - self.events.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Permanently remove all events attributed to a single conversation id.\n    pub fn purge_conversation(&mut self, conversation_id: &str) -> usize {\n        if conversation_id.is_empty() {\n            return 0;\n        }\n        let initial = self.events.len();\n        self.events\n            .retain(|event| event.conversation_id.as_deref() != Some(conversation_id));\n        initial - self.events.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear every dynamic memory event while keeping the static seed intact.\n    pub fn reset(&mut self) -> usize {\n        let initial = self.events.len();\n        self.events.clear();\n        initial\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn events(&self) -> &[MemoryEvent] {\n        &self.events\n    }\n\n    /// Rewrite every textual field of every stored event, replacing each\n    /// occurrence of `old` with `new`. This is the *write* half of the\n    /// natural-language substitution primitive (issue #529): a recall reads the\n    /// associative memory, a substitution transforms it in place — together they\n    /// give the user full, link-cli-style read+write control over memory through\n    /// ordinary language.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the total number of textual occurrences replaced across the\n    /// store. Structural keys are never touched: only the value-bearing fields\n    /// (`content`, `inputs`, `outputs`, `conversation_title`, `demo_label`) and\n    /// free-form `evidence` entries are rewritten, so a substitution can never\n    /// corrupt the document shape.\n    pub fn apply_substitution(&mut self, old: &str, new: &str) -> usize {\n        if old.is_empty() {\n            return 0;\n        }\n        let mut replacements = 0;\n        for event in &mut self.events {\n            for value in [\n                &mut event.content,\n                &mut event.inputs,\n                &mut event.outputs,\n                &mut event.conversation_title,\n                &mut event.demo_label,\n            ]\n            .into_iter()\n            .flatten()\n            {\n                replacements += replace_counting(value, old, new);\n            }\n            for entry in &mut event.evidence {\n                replacements += replace_counting(entry, old, new);\n            }\n        }\n        replacements\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        self.events.len()\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        self.events.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Render the entire memory log as a portable `demo_memory` Links\n    /// Notation document.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn export_links_notation(&self) -> String {\n        export_links_notation(&self.events)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a `demo_memory` document and replace the store's contents.\n    pub fn replace_from_links_notation(&mut self, text: &str) {\n        self.events = parse_links_notation(text);\n    }\n\n    /// Append every event parsed from a `demo_memory` document. Returns the\n    /// number of events appended.\n    pub fn import_links_notation(&mut self, text: &str) -> usize {\n        let parsed = parse_links_notation(text);\n        self.import(&parsed)\n    }\n\n    /// Load events from a file on disk. Missing file yields an empty store.\n    pub fn load_from_file<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> io::Result<Self> {\n        let path = path.as_ref();\n        if !path.exists() {\n            return Ok(Self::new());\n        }\n        let text = fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n        Ok(Self::from_events(import_full_memory(&text).events))\n    }\n\n    /// Persist the full store back to a file on disk. Creates parent\n    /// directories as needed.\n    pub fn save_to_file<P: AsRef<Path>>(&self, path: P) -> io::Result<()> {\n        let path = path.as_ref();\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            if !parent.as_os_str().is_empty() {\n                fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;\n            }\n        }\n        fs::write(path, self.export_links_notation())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replace every occurrence of `old` with `new` inside `value`, rewriting it in\n/// place and returning the number of replacements made.\nfn replace_counting(value: &mut String, old: &str, new: &str) -> usize {\n    if old.is_empty() || !value.contains(old) {\n        return 0;\n    }\n    let count = value.matches(old).count();\n    *value = value.replace(old, new);\n    count\n}\n\n/// Serialize a slice of events as a `demo_memory` Links Notation document.\n#[must_use]\npub fn export_links_notation(events: &[MemoryEvent]) -> String {\n    let mut out = String::from(ROOT_HEADER);\n    out.push('\\n');\n    for event in events {\n        format_event_into(event, &mut out);\n    }\n    out\n}\n\npub(crate) fn format_event_into(event: &MemoryEvent, out: &mut String) {\n    out.push_str(\"  event \\\"\");\n    out.push_str(&escape_value(&event.id));\n    out.push_str(\"\\\"\\n\");\n    let pairs: [(&str, Option<&str>); 11] = [\n        (\"kind\", event.kind.as_deref()),\n        (\"role\", event.role.as_deref()),\n        (\"intent\", event.intent.as_deref()),\n        (\"tool\", event.tool.as_deref()),\n        (\"inputs\", event.inputs.as_deref()),\n        (\"outputs\", event.outputs.as_deref()),\n        (\"content\", event.content.as_deref()),\n        (\"sentAt\", event.sent_at.as_deref()),\n        (\"demoLabel\", event.demo_label.as_deref()),\n        (\"conversationId\", event.conversation_id.as_deref()),\n        (\"conversationTitle\", event.conversation_title.as_deref()),\n    ];\n    for (key, value) in pairs {\n        let Some(value) = value else { continue };\n        if value.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n        out.push_str(\"    \");\n        out.push_str(key);\n        out.push_str(\" \\\"\");\n        out.push_str(&escape_value(value));\n        out.push_str(\"\\\"\\n\");\n    }\n    if !event.evidence.is_empty() {\n        let joined = event.evidence.join(\"|\");\n        out.push_str(\"    evidence \\\"\");\n        out.push_str(&escape_value(&joined));\n        out.push_str(\"\\\"\\n\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse a `demo_memory` Links Notation document into events.\npub struct ResponseUsage {\n    pub input_tokens: u32,\n    pub output_tokens: u32,\n    pub total_tokens: u32,\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_chat_completion(request: &ChatCompletionRequest) -> ChatCompletion {\n    create_chat_completion_with_solver(request, &UniversalSolver::default())\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_chat_completion_with_solver(\n    request: &ChatCompletionRequest,\n    solver: &UniversalSolver,\n) -> ChatCompletion {\n    create_chat_completion_with_solver_and_memory(request, solver, &[])\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_chat_completion_with_solver_and_memory(\n    request: &ChatCompletionRequest,\n    solver: &UniversalSolver,\n    memory_events: &[MemoryEvent],\n) -> ChatCompletion {\n    let (prompt, history) = chat_prompt_and_history(&request.messages);\n\n    match agentic_outcome(request, solver.config.agent_mode) {\n        AgenticOutcome::Refused(answer) => {\n            return chat_completion_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, answer)\n        }\n        AgenticOutcome::Planned(plan) => {\n            return chat_completion_from_plan(request, &prompt, plan, memory_events)\n        }\n        AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough => {}\n    }\n\n    if let Some(symbolic_answer) = answer_from_memory_if_requested(&prompt, &history, memory_events)\n    {\n        return chat_completion_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, symbolic_answer);\n    }\n\n    let mut symbolic_answer = solver.solve_with_history(&prompt, &history);\n    apply_retained_amendments(&prompt, &mut symbolic_answer, memory_events);\n    chat_completion_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, symbolic_answer)\n}\n\n/// The deterministic agentic decision for a tool-bearing request. Shared by every\n/// OpenAI-shaped surface — `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/messages`, and\n/// `/v1/responses` — so the issue #468 agentic loop behaves identically across all\n/// three. Pure and deterministic: same `request` + `agent_mode` ⇒ same outcome.\nenum AgenticOutcome {\n    /// Tools were requested but refused — either agent mode is off (the real\n    /// guard) or a requested tool failed the per-tool permission gate.\n    Refused(SymbolicAnswer),\n    /// The planner produced the next deterministic step (`tool_calls` or final).\n    Planned(AgenticPlan),\n    /// Not an agentic request — no tools were requested, or the task is not one\n    /// the planner recognises — so the caller should answer symbolically.\n    Fallthrough,\n}\n\n/// Decide the [`AgenticOutcome`] for `request` under the given `agent_mode`,\n/// applying the two gates (agent mode, then per-tool permissions) before letting\n/// [`plan_chat_step`] drive the loop. An unrecognised task yields\n/// [`AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough`] so ordinary chat stays untouched.\nfn agentic_outcome(request: &ChatCompletionRequest, agent_mode: bool) -> AgenticOutcome {\n    let trace = std::env::var(\"FORMAL_AI_TRACE_REQUESTS\").as_deref() == Ok(\"1\");\n    if !request.requests_tool_execution() {\n        if trace {\n            eprintln!(\"[trace] agentic_outcome: fallthrough (no tool execution requested)\");\n        }\n        return AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough;\n    }\n    if !agent_mode {\n        if trace {\n            eprintln!(\"[trace] agentic_outcome: refused (agent_mode off)\");\n        }\n        return AgenticOutcome::Refused(tool_call_refusal_answer());\n    }\n    let owned_names = request.requested_tool_names();\n    if trace {\n        eprintln!(\n            \"[trace] agentic_outcome: {} advertised tools: {owned_names:?}\",\n            owned_names.len()\n        );\n    }\n    if let Some(denial) = agentic_tool_permission_denial(&owned_names) {\n        if trace {\n            eprintln!(\"[trace] agentic_outcome: refused by permission gate: {denial:?}\");\n        }\n        return AgenticOutcome::Refused(tool_permission_refusal_answer(&denial));\n    }\n    // Agent mode with tools permitted: the deterministic agentic planner drives\n    // the loop, emitting `tool_calls` or a final answer. An unrecognised task\n    // yields `None` and falls through to the solver.\n    let tool_names: Vec<&str> = owned_names.iter().map(String::as_str).collect();\n    let outcome = plan_chat_step(&request.messages, &tool_names)\n        .map_or(AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough, AgenticOutcome::Planned);\n    if trace {\n        match &outcome {\n            AgenticOutcome::Planned(plan) => eprintln!(\"[trace] agentic_outcome: planned {plan:?}\"),\n            AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough => {\n                eprintln!(\"[trace] agentic_outcome: fallthrough (task unrecognised)\");\n            }\n            AgenticOutcome::Refused(_) => {}\n        }\n    }\n    outcome\n}\n\n/// Build a chat completion from a deterministic [`AgenticPlan`]. A\n/// [`AgenticPlan::ToolCalls`] plan emits an assistant turn carrying `tool_calls`\n/// with `finish_reason: \"tool_calls\"`; a [`AgenticPlan::Final`] plan emits plain\n/// assistant text with `finish_reason: \"stop\"`.\nfn chat_completion_from_plan(\n    request: &ChatCompletionRequest,\n    prompt: &str,\n    plan: AgenticPlan,\n    memory_events: &[MemoryEvent],\n) -> ChatCompletion {\n    let model = resolved_request_model(request.model.as_deref());\n    let prompt_tokens = estimate_tokens(prompt);\n\n    let (message, finish_reason, completion_tokens) = match plan {\n        AgenticPlan::ToolCalls(calls) => {\n            let completion_tokens = calls\n                .iter()\n                .map(|call| {\n                    estimate_tokens(&call.tool).saturating_add(estimate_tokens(&call.arguments))\n                })\n                .sum();\n            let tool_calls = calls\n                .into_iter()\n                .enumerate()\n                .map(|(index, call)| {\n                    let seed = format!(\"{prompt}|{index}|{}|{}\", call.tool, call.arguments);\n                    ToolCall::function(stable_id(\"call\", &seed), call.tool, call.arguments)\n                })\n                .collect();\n            (\n                ChatMessage::assistant_tool_calls(tool_calls),\n                String::from(\"tool_calls\"),\n                completion_tokens,\n            )\n        }\n        AgenticPlan::Final(answer) => {\n            let answer = amended_answer(prompt, &answer, memory_events);\n            let completion_tokens = estimate_tokens(&answer);\n            (\n                ChatMessage::assistant(answer),\n                String::from(\"stop\"),\n                completion_tokens,\n            )\n        }\n    };\n\n    ChatCompletion {\n        id: stable_id(\"chatcmpl\", prompt),\n        object: String::from(\"chat.completion\"),\n        created: 0,\n        model,\n        choices: vec![ChatChoice {\n            index: 0,\n            message,\n            finish_reason,\n        }],\n        usage: TokenUsage {\n            prompt_tokens,\n            completion_tokens,\n            total_tokens: prompt_tokens.saturating_add(completion_tokens),\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn chat_completion_from_symbolic(\n    request: &ChatCompletionRequest,\n    prompt: &str,\n    symbolic_answer: SymbolicAnswer,\n) -> ChatCompletion {\n    let model = resolved_request_model(request.model.as_deref());\n    let prompt_tokens = estimate_tokens(prompt);\n    let completion_tokens = estimate_tokens(&symbolic_answer.answer);\n    let thinking_steps = symbolic_answer.thinking_steps;\n    let reasoning = render_thinking_steps(&thinking_steps);\n    let mut message = ChatMessage::assistant(symbolic_answer.answer);\n    message.thinking_steps = thinking_steps;\n    message.reasoning_content.clone_from(&reasoning);\n    message.reasoning = reasoning;\n\n    ChatCompletion {\n        id: stable_id(\"chatcmpl\", prompt),\n        object: String::from(\"chat.completion\"),\n        created: 0,\n        model,\n        choices: vec![ChatChoice {\n            index: 0,\n            message,\n            finish_reason: String::from(\"stop\"),\n        }],\n        usage: TokenUsage {\n            prompt_tokens,\n            completion_tokens,\n            total_tokens: prompt_tokens.saturating_add(completion_tokens),\n        },\n    }\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_response(request: &ResponsesRequest) -> ResponseObject {\n    let prompt = response_prompt(request);\n    let symbolic_answer = FormalAiEngine.answer(&prompt);\n    response_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, symbolic_answer)\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_response_with_solver(\n    request: &ResponsesRequest,\n    solver: &UniversalSolver,\n) -> ResponseObject {\n    create_response_with_solver_and_memory(request, solver, &[])\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn create_response_with_solver_and_memory(\n    request: &ResponsesRequest,\n    solver: &UniversalSolver,\n    memory_events: &[MemoryEvent],\n) -> ResponseObject {\n    let prompt = response_prompt(request);\n    let chat_request = request.to_chat_completion_request();\n    match agentic_outcome(&chat_request, solver.config.agent_mode) {\n        AgenticOutcome::Refused(answer) => return response_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, answer),\n        AgenticOutcome::Planned(plan) => {\n            return response_from_plan(request, &prompt, plan, memory_events)\n        }\n        AgenticOutcome::Fallthrough => {}\n    }\n    let (memory_prompt, history) = chat_prompt_and_history(&chat_request.messages);\n    let memory_prompt = if memory_prompt.trim().is_empty() {\n        prompt.as_str()\n    } else {\n        memory_prompt.as_str()\n    };\n    if let Some(symbolic_answer) =\n        answer_from_memory_if_requested(memory_prompt, &history, memory_events)\n    {\n        return response_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, symbolic_answer);\n    }\n    let mut symbolic_answer = solver.solve(&prompt);\n    apply_retained_amendments(&prompt, &mut symbolic_answer, memory_events);\n    response_from_symbolic(request, &prompt, symbolic_answer)\n}\n\n/// Build a Responses object from a deterministic [`AgenticPlan`] — the Responses\n/// mirror of [`chat_completion_from_plan`]. A [`AgenticPlan::ToolCalls`] plan emits\n/// `function_call` output items (the same stable `call` ids the chat surface uses);\n/// a [`AgenticPlan::Final`] plan emits a single assistant message.\nfn response_from_plan(\n    request: &ResponsesRequest,\n    prompt: &str,\n    plan: AgenticPlan,\n    memory_events: &[MemoryEvent],\n) -> ResponseObject {\n    let model = resolved_request_model(request.model.as_deref());\n    let input_tokens = estimate_tokens(prompt);\n\n    let (output, output_tokens) = match plan {\n        AgenticPlan::ToolCalls(calls) => {\n            let mut items = Vec::with_capacity(calls.len());\n            let mut output_tokens = 0u32;\n            for (index, call) in calls.into_iter().enumerate() {\n                let tool = call.tool;\n                let planned_arguments = call.arguments;\n                let seed = format!(\"{prompt}|{index}|{tool}|{planned_arguments}\");\n                let arguments =\n                    response_arguments_for_tool(&request.tools, &tool, planned_arguments);\n                output_tokens = output_tokens.saturating_add(\n                    estimate_tokens(&tool).saturating_add(estimate_tokens(&arguments)),\n                );\n                items.push(ResponseOutputItem::FunctionCall(ResponseFunctionToolCall {\n                    id: stable_id(\"fc\", &seed),\n                    kind: function_call_kind(),\n                    call_id: stable_id(\"call\", &seed),\n                    name: tool,\n                    arguments,\n                    status: String::from(\"completed\"),\n                }));\n            }\n            (items, output_tokens)\n        }\n        AgenticPlan::Final(answer) => {\n            let answer = amended_answer(prompt, &answer, memory_events);\n            let output_tokens = estimate_tokens(&answer);\n            let message = ResponseOutputItem::Message(ResponseOutputMessage {\n                id: stable_id(\"msg\", &answer),\n                kind: String::from(\"message\"),\n                role: String::from(\"assistant\"),\n                content: vec![ResponseOutputContent {\n                    kind: String::from(\"output_text\"),\n                    text: answer,\n                }],\n                thinking_steps: Vec::new(),\n            });\n            (vec![message], output_tokens)\n        }\n    };\n\n    ResponseObject {\n        id: stable_id(\"resp\", prompt),\n        object: String::from(\"response\"),\n        created_at: 0,\n        status: String::from(\"completed\"),\n        model,\n        output,\n        usage: ResponseUsage {\n            input_tokens,\n            output_tokens,\n            total_tokens: input_tokens.saturating_add(output_tokens),\n        },\n        evidence_links: Vec::new(),\n//! Agent-CLI recipe for auditing and generalizing the issue-#540 dreaming loop.\n//!\n//! The audit is intentionally derived from the grounded dreaming recipe rather\n//! than from a second prose checklist. Driving this recipe through Agent CLI\n//! writes the gap analysis, reads it back, and returns it as the final answer.\n\nuse std::fmt::Write as _;\n\npub const DREAMING_AUDIT_PATH: &str = \"dreaming-gap-analysis.lino\";\n\npub const DREAMING_AUDIT_TASK: &str =\n    \"Audit issue 540 dreaming through Formal AI: inspect the grounded dreaming meta-algorithm, \\\n     identify every implementation gap from amendment application through replay, pattern \\\n     learning, storage consent, and idle runtime, then record the generalization that resolves \\\n     each gap as Links Notation.\";\n\nconst RECIPE: &str = include_str!(\"../../data/meta/dreaming-recipe.lino\");\nconst CUES: &str = include_str!(\"../../data/meta/dreaming-cues.lino\");\n\nstruct GapResolution {\n    gap: &'static str,\n    evidence_step: &'static str,\n    generalization: &'static str,\n}\n\nconst RESOLUTIONS: [GapResolution; 7] = [\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"stored amendments were not consumed by future tasks\",\n        evidence_step: \"apply_future_tasks\",\n        generalization: \"represent amendments as structured data and apply every matching retained rule at the shared protocol answer boundary\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"topic matching alone claimed that a specific was reproducible\",\n        evidence_step: \"replay_candidates\",\n        generalization: \"derive candidate tasks and require normalized output replay before granting covered status\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"requirement extraction did not test the meta-algorithm or discover structures\",\n        evidence_step: \"mine_patterns\",\n        generalization: \"simulate frequent-topic tasks and retain repeated task structures independently of requirement cue words\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"storage pressure depended on optional static inputs\",\n        evidence_step: \"measure_real_storage\",\n        generalization: \"measure the filesystem containing memory and include the actual next write byte count\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"automatic cleanup had no persisted user decision or migration prompt\",\n        evidence_step: \"idle_consent_runtime\",\n        generalization: \"persist acceptance and refusal, remove only after acceptance, and surface larger-storage pressure\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"background dreaming existed only in desktop timers\",\n        evidence_step: \"idle_consent_runtime\",\n        generalization: \"run the same loop in the core server and guard both runtimes with foreground-idle signals and low process priority\",\n    },\n    GapResolution {\n        gap: \"English-only code cues and graph terminology limited generalization\",\n        evidence_step: \"learn_requirements\",\n        generalization: \"load multilingual cues from extensible data and describe the representation consistently as memory links\",\n    },\n];\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn is_dreaming_audit_task(prompt: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = prompt.to_lowercase();\n    lower.contains(\"dreaming\")\n        && (lower.contains(\"issue 540\")\n            || lower.contains(\"gap analysis\")\n            || lower.contains(\"audit\"))\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn render_document() -> String {\n    let recipe_steps = RECIPE\n        .lines()\n        .filter(|line| line.trim() == \"record_type \\\"meta_step\\\"\")\n        .count();\n    let cue_count = CUES\n        .lines()\n        .filter(|line| line.trim_start().starts_with(\"cue \\\"\"))\n        .count();\n    let mut out = String::from(\"dreaming_gap_analysis\\n\");\n    out.push_str(\"  record_type \\\"agent_cli_gap_analysis\\\"\\n\");\n    out.push_str(\"  issue \\\"540\\\"\\n\");\n    let _ = writeln!(out, \"  grounded_recipe_steps \\\"{recipe_steps}\\\"\");\n    let _ = writeln!(out, \"  multilingual_cues \\\"{cue_count}\\\"\");\n    out.push_str(\"  conclusion \\\"all identified gaps resolved by reusable stages\\\"\\n\");\n    for (index, resolution) in RESOLUTIONS.iter().enumerate() {\n        let _ = writeln!(out, \"  resolution_{:02}\", index + 1);\n        field(&mut out, \"gap\", resolution.gap);\n        field(&mut out, \"evidence_step\", resolution.evidence_step);\n        field(&mut out, \"generalization\", resolution.generalization);\n        out.push_str(\"    status \\\"implemented_and_tested\\\"\\n\");\n    }\n    out\n}\n\n#[must_use]\npub fn final_answer(document: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\n        \"Audited the grounded issue-#540 dreaming meta-algorithm through Formal AI. The recipe \\\n         now has {} stages and the audit records {} implementation gaps together with the \\\n         reusable generalization that resolves each one.\\n\\nGenerated document \\\n         ({DREAMING_AUDIT_PATH}):\\n\\n{}\",\n        RECIPE\n            .lines()\n            .filter(|line| line.trim() == \"record_type \\\"meta_step\\\"\")\n            .count(),\n        RESOLUTIONS.len(),\n        document.trim_end(),\n    )\n}\n\nfn field(out: &mut String, name: &str, value: &str) {\n    let escaped = value.replace('\\\\', \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', \"\\\\\\\"\");\n    let _ = writeln!(out, \"    {name} \\\"{escaped}\\\"\");\n}\n    classify_tool(name)\n}\n\n/// Plan the next agentic step from the conversation so far and the tool names the\n/// CLI advertised.\n///\n/// Returns [`None`] when the latest user turn is neither of the recipes the\n/// planner knows (formalize a text — issue #468, or make a meaning more detailed\n/// — issue #538) — the server then falls back to its ordinary solver text, so\n/// unrelated requests are untouched.\n#[must_use]\npub fn plan_chat_step(messages: &[ChatMessage], tool_names: &[&str]) -> Option<AgenticPlan> {\n    let task = latest_user_text(messages)?;\n    // The self-AST recipe is checked first because it is the most specific router\n    // (it requires both an AST/CST intent word *and* a self-reference). A self-AST\n    // request legitimately mentions \"Links Notation\" as its output format, which\n    // would otherwise be captured by the broad formalization keyword match below.\n    // The self-healing recipe is checked before self-AST: both are self-inspection\n    // recipes, but self-healing has its own dedicated keywords (self-heal, repair\n    // case, auto-learning) that never overlap the AST/CST keywords, so ordering only\n    // guards against a request that names both.\n    if self_heal::is_self_heal_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_self_heal_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    if dreaming_audit::is_dreaming_audit_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_dreaming_audit_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    if self_ast::is_self_ast_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_self_ast_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    // The whole-repository source-graph recipe: checked alongside the other\n    // self-inspection recipes and before formalization, because its request\n    // legitimately names \"links\" (its output format), which the broad\n    // formalization keyword match below would otherwise capture.\n    if source_graph::is_source_graph_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_source_graph_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    // The learning-ledger recipe: the promotion step that follows an approved repair\n    // case. Checked after self-healing (which owns the \"auto learning\" keywords) and\n    // before formalization, since its request legitimately names \"Links Notation\".\n    if ledger::is_ledger_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_ledger_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    // The grounded self-explanation recipe: answers \"how does Formal AI work?\" from\n    // real source/data/test artifacts. Checked alongside the other self-inspection\n    // recipes and before formalization, since its request legitimately names \"Links\n    // Notation\" as the output format its document is rendered in.\n    if explain::is_explain_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_explain_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    // The user-initiated self-change recipe: turns a natural-language \"change Formal AI\n    // itself\" request into a reviewable pull request through the same human-gated loop.\n    // Checked alongside the other self-referential recipes and before formalization,\n    // since its request legitimately names \"Links Notation\" as the output format.\n    if change_request::is_change_request_task(&task) {\n        return Some(plan_change_request_step(messages, tool_names));\n    }\n    // The general repair-classification recipe: given an arbitrary failure trace, decide\n    // whether the repair is a solver method, a data record, or a test, and compose the\n    // grounded, human-gated strategy for each class. Checked alongside the other\n    // self-referential recipes and before formalization, since its request legitimately\n                && requirement.statement.contains(\"LaTeX\"))\n    );\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn dreaming_generalizes_requirements_into_meta_algorithm_amendments() {\n    // The learned requirement must be baked into a meta-algorithm amendment that\n    // covers the specific test-run records it can reproduce.\n    let events = vec![\n        requirement_event(\"req-1\", \"latex\", \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\"),\n        verified_task_run_event(\n step\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn dreaming_marks_only_replay_verified_specifics_as_covered() {\n    let events = vec![\n        requirement_event(\n            \"req-1\",\n            \"latex\",\n   std::process::id()\n    ));\n    let preference_path = memory_path.with_file_name(format!(\n        \"{}.auto-free-space\",\n        memory_path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy()\n    ));\n assert!(snapshot.free_bytes <= snapshot.capacity_bytes);\n    assert!(!auto_free_space_enabled(&memory_path));\n    persist_auto_free_space_choice(&memory_path, true).expect(\"persist opt-in\");\n    assert!(auto_free_space_enabled(&memory_path));\n    persist_auto_free_space_choice(&memory_path, false).expect(\"persist opt-out\");\n    assert!(!auto_free_space_enabled(&memory_path));\n\n    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(preference_path);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn dreaming_requirement_learning_uses_multilingual_data_cues() {\n    let events = vec![\n        requirement_event(\n            \"req-ru\",\n store.save_to_file(&memory_path).expect(\"write fixture\");\n    persist_auto_free_space_choice(&memory_path, false).expect(\"persist default-off\");\n\n    let outcome =    },\n    ];\n    let plan = plan_memory_dreaming(\n        &events,\n        &DreamingConfig {\n            storage_capacity_bytes: Some(1_000),\n            free_bytes: Some(0),\n            ..DreamingConfig::default()\n        },\n    );\n\n    assert_eq!(plan.event_usage(\"seed-unused\"), Some(0)
[2026-07-11T15:41:59.129Z] [INFO] );\n    assert!(plan.event_usage(\"seed-used\").unwrap_or_default() > 0);\n    let unused_position = plan\n        .actions\n        .iter()\n        .position(|action| action.event_id == \"seed-unused\")\n        .expect(\"unused seed cache selected\");\n    let used_position = plan\n        .actions\n        .iter()\n        .position(|action| requirement_event(\"req-1\", \"latex\", \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\"),\n        verified_task_run_event(\n            \"run-1\",\n            \"latex\",\n            &\"proof render specifics \".repeat(20),\n            \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\",\n        ),\n        recomputable_event(\"unrelated-cache\", &\"unrelated cached source \".repeat(5)),\n    ];\n    let config = DreamingConfig {\n        storage_capacity_bytes: Some(1_000),\n        free_bytes: Some(0),\n        incoming_bytes: 0,\n        ..DreamingConfig::default()\n    };\n\n    let plan = assert!(!plan.actions.iter().any(|action| action.event_id == \"req-1\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn applying_dreaming_plan_bakes_amendments_and_is_idempotent() {\n    // Applying the plan materializes each amendment as a retained learning event;\n    // re-applying an unchanged plan must not duplicate it.\n    let mut store = MemoryStore::from_events(vec![\n        requirement_event(\"req-1\", \"latex\", \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\"),\n        verified_task_run_event(\n            \"run-1\",\n            \"latex\",\n            \"proof render pass 1\",\n            \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\",\n        ),\n        verified_task_run_event(\n            \"run-2\",\n            \"latex\",\n            \"proof render pass 2\",\n            \"Always compile proofs with LaTeX.\",\n        ),\n    ]);\n    let plan = plan_memory_dreaming(store.events(), &DreamingConfig::default());\n\n    let outcome = apply_dreaming_plan(&mut store, &plan);\n    assert_eq!(outcome.learned_amendments, 1);\n    let amendment_count = store\n        .events()\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|event| event.kind.as_deref() == Some(\"meta_algorithm_amendment\"))\n        .count();\n    assert_eq!(amendment_count, 1);\n\n    // Re-planning against the mutated store keeps the amendment retained and does\n    // not append a second copy.\n    let replan = plan_memory_dreaming(store.events(), &DreamingConfig::default());\n    let second = apply_dreaming_plan(&mut store, &replan);\n    assert_eq!(second.learned_amendments, 0);\n    let amendment_count_after = store\n        .events()\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|event| event.kind.as_deref() == Some(\"meta_algorithm_amendment\"))\n        .count();\n    assert_eq!(amendment_count_after, 1);\n}\n\nfn requirement_event(id: &str, topic: &str, statement: &str) -> MemoryEvent {\n    MemoryEvent {\n        id: String::from(id),\n        kind: Some(String::from(\"message\")),\n        role: Some(String::from(\"user\")),\n        content: Some(String::from(statement)),\n        conversation_title: Some(String::from(topic)),\n        ..MemoryEvent::default()\n    }\n}\n\nfn verified_task_run_event(id: &str, topic: &str, input: &str, requirement: &str) -> MemoryEvent {\n    let solved = UniversalSolver::default().solve(input).answer;\n    MemoryEvent {\n        id: String::from(id),\n        kind: Some(String::from(\"test_run\")),\n        role: Some(String::from(\"assistant\")),\n        content: Some(String::from(input)),\n        inputs: Some(String::from(input)),\n        outputs: Some(format!(\n            \"{solved}\\n\\nLearned standing requirement ({topic}): {requirement}\"\n        )),\n        conversation_title: Some(String::from(topic)),\n        ..MemoryEvent::default()\n    }\n}\n\nfn recomputable_event(id: &str, payload: &str) -> MemoryEvent {\n for the progress — the deterministic planner, durability classes, and the grounded recipe are a good skeleton. But after re-reading the code against issue #540, issue #494, and my earlier comment (https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/645#issuecomment-4923226122), several core requirements are **claimed in docs but not actually implemented**. This PR is not complete until every item below is fully wired, tested, and documented. Please continue in this same PR, with unlimited time and context, until all of it is totally done.\n\n## 1. Amendments must actually change how tasks are solved (the core requirement)\n\n`meta_algorithm_amendment` events are written by `apply_dreaming_plan`, but **nothing ever reads them**: the solver (`src/solver_handlers/`), the server, and the meta-algorithm never consult amendments when answering or solving. Today the \"baked in\" requirement is a stored string, not changed behavior.\n\n- [ ] When solving a task, load retained `meta_algorithm_amendment` events, match them to the task's topic, and **apply the learned standing requirements to the produced solution/answer**.\n- [ ] Add an end-to-end test: user states a requirement on topic X → dreaming generalizes it → a *new* task on topic X is solved **with the requirement applied, without the user repeating it**. That test is the definition of done for \"the user never has to repeat himself.\"\n- [ ] The amendment must amend the *meta-algorithm as data* (our grounded, self-defining direction — like `data/meta/*.lino` recipes), not live only as a free-text rule.\n\n## 2. Generalization must be verified as working before specifics are forgotten\n\nIssue #540 is explicit: \"Generalization should be treated as successful, as given input generalized algorithm can produce specific algorithm, as soon as it is working, specific algorithm can be forgotten.\" Currently `covered_by_amendment` is set by mere topic match + reclaimability — the amendment is never asked to reproduce the specific record before `ForgetCoveredSpecific` deletes it.\n\n- [ ] Before a specific task/test-run record may be forgotten, **verify the retained amendment can actually re-derive/reproduce it** (replay the generalized rule against the specific's inputs and compare). Only verified-covered specifics are eligible for `ForgetCoveredSpecific`.\n- [ ] Unverified specifics must fall back to normal eviction rules, never to \"covered\" priority.\n- [ ] Tests for both the verified-and-forgotten path and the failed-verification-and-retained path.\n\n## 3. Dreaming must actually dream: find tasks on frequent topics and test the meta-algorithm on them\n\nMy comment asked to \"find tasks on the topic, make sure Formal AI own meta algorithm is general enough to solve these\" and to dream \"about new tasks.\" Today dreaming only counts topic frequency and lifts keyword-cued sentences. It never finds or attempts a task.\n\n- [ ] During dreaming, for the most-used topics, derive candidate tasks from stored experience (prior tasks/test runs on the topic) and **run the meta-algorithm against them** (dry-run/simulation, still low priority).\n- [ ] When solving fails or violates a learned requirement, dreaming must **improve the meta-algorithm by generalization** and record the amendment — that is the auto-learning loop, not just extraction.\n- [ ] Go beyond keyword cues: find **patterns, regularities, trends, laws** across events (issue #540 wording) — e.g., recurring structures across tasks on a topic — and record them as retained learning. Keyword cues in English only (\"always\", \"must\", …) are not the highest possible potential.\n\n## 4. Issue #494 must be fully applied and closed by this PR\n\nIssue #540 says \"the resulting pull request will close both tasks.\" The PR only closes #540, and key #494 behaviors are stubs:\n\n- [ ] Add `Closes #494` to the PR description and make it true.\n- [ ] **Measure real storage capacity/free space** (statvfs/df or Node equivalent) instead of requiring `--storage-capacity-bytes`/env vars; without this the 20% reserve and pressure path never trigger in any real deployment. Manual overrides can stay for tests.\n- [ ] \"By default system should free nothing, but when not enough space we should **ask the user** if to enable auto-free-space\" — implement the actual ask: a desktop UI consent prompt (and an interactive CLI prompt) that enables the auto-free-space algorithm, persisted as the user's choice. Silent plan-only runs are not an ask.\n- [ ] \"When even our algorithm cannot free enough space, we should **ask the user to migrate AI memory to bigger storage**\" — `requires_bigger_storage` must surface to the user in the desktop UI, not only in CLI plan text.\n- [ ] Usage counting must cover **cached and seed data** (\"even in seed data\"), not only the `MemoryEvent` log, and freeing must stay minimal (\"only enough space to store next required links/data/files\") — verify `incoming_bytes` is fed from the real next write, not always 0.\n\n## 5. Execute this task through Formal AI via Agent CLI, and document it\n\nThe issue requires the task itself to be driven through Formal AI connected to Agent CLI, improving algorithms by generalization wherever Formal AI falls short. The case study contains no record of this at all.\n\n- [ ] Actually drive Formal AI via Agent CLI on (parts of) this task; record the sessions/transcripts in `docs/case-studies/issue-540/`.\n- [ ] For every step Formal AI could not do, document the gap and the **generalization added** so it can next time — that gap analysis is a deliverable of this PR.\n- [ ] Extend the case study's solution plans with the survey of known existing components/libraries for each requirement (memory GC, deduplication, idle scheduling, pattern mining), as originally requested.\n\n## 6. Background dreaming everywhere, truly idle, truly low priority\n\n- [ ] Dreaming runs only in the Electron desktop scheduler and as a manual CLI command. Wire background dreaming into the core (server/agent) runtime too, so it's on by default wherever Formal AI runs, per issue #540.\n- [ ] Add idle detection: the docs claim \"while idle,\" but the scheduler is a fixed 60s + 6h timer regardless of user activity. Dream when the app is actually idle, and yield when foreground work arrives (`nice -19` alone is not idle-awareness; also cover Windows, which currently gets no priority lowering at all).\n\n## 7. Consistency with our conventions\n\n- [ ] REQUIREMENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and `dreaming-recipe.lino` say \"memory graph\" — per our terminology everything is links; please align the wording (\"memory links\" / \"links store\").\n- [ ] Requirement/topic extraction should not be English-keyword-bound given the product is multilingual; at minimum, ground the cue list as data (`data/meta/`) so it is extensible, and note the generalization path.\n\nAs before: follow the contributing and testing guidelines, vision and roadmap; every checkbox above needs implementation + tests + updated requirements traceability (R540-xx), and anything obsolete or contradicting generalization that you touch must be fixed.
